text:
Very little period activity reported in the crude sector once again as the market remained subdued.
Little to report in the clean market aside from a few MR fixtures to report.
Image reference: 2021-07-07_weekly-tanker-time-charter-estimates-july-07-2021_img2.jpg

The crude market remained negative this week with very little signs of interest in period enquiry. One-year rates for VLCCs continued to weaken, down by $500 this week to an average of $25,000/pdpr.
Limited fresh business to report on the clean side and as the market remained under pressure. MR rates for one-year remained at an average of $12,000/pdpr.
Image reference: 2021-07-14_weekly-tanker-time-charter-estimates-july-14-2021_img2.jpg

Despite the announcement from OPEC+ that oil production will increase in the coming months, to meet rising demand, the crude market hasremained flat once again this week. Aside from some interest in the suezmax sector, there has been very little to report in the way of period activity.
Another quiet week for clean with very little to report in the way of timecharter fixtures.
Oil prices have dipped this week, following the news of increased crude production from OPEC+.
Image reference: 2021-07-21_weekly-tanker-time-charter-estimates-july-21-2021_img2.jpg

Little change in the crude market from last week, although there have been rumors of some short-term period fixtures from oil majors as companiescontinue to stay away from longer periods due to ongoing market uncertainty.
Limited fresh business to report in the clean market as the short-term outlook continues to look bleak.
Oil prices have moved up to recover from last week's dip.
Image reference: 2021-07-28_weekly-tanker-time-charter-estimates-july-28-2021_img2.jpg

Limited activity for crude once again this week with period rate assessments for VLCCSfor one year moving slightly lower to an average of $22,500/pdpr.
Some improvement in sentiment for the MR market this week, although rate levels remain low, boosted by the spot market that has moved up to the highest levels in two months with a few short-term fixtures reported.
Oil prices fell mid-week amid concerns over the spread of the Delta variant in the US and China.
Image reference: 2021-08-04_weekly-tanker-time-charter-estimates-august-04-2021_img2.jpg

Very little period activity reported in the crude sector once again this week, aside from one or two long term deals reported in the suezmax and aframax sectors. On the whole the market remains subdued, and rates have moved sideways.
On the clean side, some very short-term period fixtures were reported in the MR and LR1 sectors although rates remained flat.
Oil prices steadied mid-week as the IEA warned of a slowdown in demand recovery due to the spread of the Delta variant.
Image reference: 2021-08-11_weekly-tanker-time-charter-estimates-august-11-2021_img2.jpg

Some very short term aframax fixtures reported in the crude market although rates remain flatas there is still a way to go until this sector sees some form of recovery.
Very little to report on the clean side in terms of fixture activity this week, with market players hoping for some activity towards the end of the month as the summer holiday season winds down.
Oil prices have eased slightly this week as further lockdowns in Asia weaken demand sentiment.
Image reference: 2021-08-18_weekly-tanker-time-charter-estimates-august-18-2021_img2.jpg

With a grim outlook for the rest of the year, it's becoming increasingly difficult to be optimistic for this sector. Some period interest in the Aframax sector this week as port delays in Asia have created some demand for this class.
In the clean market, some MR fixtures have been reported that are keeping this sector ticking over but on the whole very limited activity to report.
Image reference: 2021-08-25_weekly-tanker-time-charter-estimates-august-25-2021_img2.jpg

Little change in the crude market from last week, some short-term period interest has been reported however with rate levels remaining low, there is little incentive for owners to consider longer periods as they hold out for an improvement in rates.
Limited fresh business to report in the clean market, aside from one or two MR fixtures, as this sector continues to struggle from a lack of demand.
Image reference: 2021-09-01_weekly-tanker-time-charter-estimates-september-01-2021_img2.jpg

In the crude spot market, rates firmed slightly as more cargo entered the market but this has yet to have an impacton the period market where rates remained flat.
Limited fresh period business to report on the clean side as the market remained at low levels.
Oil prices fell mid-week on concerns over weak demand in the US and Asia and pressure from a strong dollar. Ongoing production outages on the US Gulf Coast capped losses.
Image reference: 2021-09-08_weekly-tanker-time-charter-estimates-september-08-2021_img2.jpg

A few short-term period fixtures reported in the crude sector this week and this has given the market a slight sense of optimism.
Little fresh business to report on the clean side this week with rate levels so low and some owners prepared to wait, ever optimistic about some form of recovery in Q4.
Oil prices have climbed mid-week as industry data shows a larger than expected drawdown in US crude inventories and growing expectations that demand levels will rise once again as the vaccination program broadens.
Image reference: 2021-09-15_weekly-tanker-time-charter-estimates-september-15-2021_img2.jpg

Some VLCC fixtures have been reported in the crude sector this week but this has had little impact on rates as sentiment remains low.
Limited activity in the clean sector this week and very little to report in terms of period fixtures.
Oil prices climbed this week as data showed US crude stocks falling more than expected following the two hurricanes highlighting tight supply as demand improves.
Image reference: 2021-09-22_weekly-tanker-time-charter-estimates-september-22-2021_img2.jpg

Spot market rates have picked up somewhat on certain routes. If we were to pick any sector of the crude marketfor potential to improve, it would be the VLCCS as there has been a little bit of interest from charterers for one-year TC. Uncertainty over the future is holding them back but as soon as there is some clarity the VLCCs will be the first to move.
Very little to report on the clean side in terms of fixture activity.
Oil prices climbed above $80/barrel for the first time in three years off the back of the energy crisis in Europe.
Image reference: 2021-09-29_weekly-tanker-time-charter-estimates-september-29-2021_img2.jpg

In the crude sector we have seen some short term timecharter fixtures reported this week across all sectorsas expectations of cold winter and the energy supply shortage in China has boosted sentiment somewhat.
In the clean market some short-term MR fixtures have been reported this week that are keeping the market ticking over as this sector continues to struggle from a lack of demand.
Brent hit a 3-year high and US Crude jumped up to 2014 levels his week as OPEC+ decided to stick to planned output increases rather than raising it further.
Image reference: 2021-10-06_weekly-tanker-time-charter-estimates-october-06-2021_img2.jpg

A flat week for the crude market and although the global energy supply shortage has boosted sentiment somewhat, this has yet to translate to any upwards momentum in rates.
On the clean side, there has been some demand for LR2s this week with interest shown from charterers for both eco and non eco vessels, one-year rates for this sector remained at an estimated $16,500/pdpr.
Oil prices surged to the highest levels in three years fueled by hopes of rebounding global demand and energy shortages in key economies such as China.
Image reference: 2021-10-13_weekly-tanker-time-charter-estimates-october-13-2021_img2.jpg

Some increased interest in the VLCC period market this week although with a gap between the levels charterers are offering and what owners are willing to accept, there has been little to report in terms of fixtures.
On the clean side, interest in the LR2 sector has continued this week and on the MRs rate sentiment has firmed slightly providing some optimism.
Oil prices continued to surge this week reaching multi year highs thanks to the global energy supply crunch.
Image reference: 2021-10-20_weekly-tanker-time-charter-estimates-october-20-2021_img2.jpg

In the crude market slightly more timecharter interest reported this week as the market showed some small signs of positivity. The main focus of interest has been in the suezmax sector with demand for all periods.
The clean market continues to underperform although with owners rate ideas are more positive than charterers and therefore a gap that needs to be bridged between bid and offer.
Image reference: 2021-10-27_weekly-tanker-time-charter-estimates-october-27-2021_img2.jpg

In the crude sector we have seen timecharter rates for most sectors remain steady this week as enquiry increased. Some period interest reported for the suezmax sector with fixtures recorded largely short-term, ranging from four months to one year.
There has been some interest in the clean sector this week, but little concluded in the way of fixtures. Rates for the most part remained stable with one-year rates for MRs ticking up slightly to an average of $12,200/pdpr.
Image reference: 2021-11-03_weekly-tanker-time-charter-estimates-november-03-2021_img2.jpg

There seems to be slightly more interest in the crude sector this week and sentiment is growing that we are at the beginning of an upwards period. A handful of short-term VLCC period fixtures were reported and rates moved higher with the one-year average for VLCCs up $1,000 to an estimated $22,500/pdpr.
On the clean side, there has been little to report in the way of fixtures although rates for MRs increased slightly across all periods with one-year periods now approximately $12,250/pdpr.
Oil prices have risen to a two-week high as the US eases travel restrictions and the American Petroleum Institute (API) reported its first crude oil inventory draw in six weeks.
Image reference: 2021-11-10_weekly-tanker-time-charter-estimates-november-10-2021_img2.jpg

In the crude market a rebound is slowly emerging and we are seeing more and more interest in long term deals of around three years. The average rate for three years is up $1000 to an average of $36,000/pdpr.
On the clean side, there has been interest in all sectors although the short-term rates have strengthened, for most periods rate-levels have remained flat.
Although oil prices have fallen from the highs of last week, prices remain above $80/barrel as demand for oil strengthens as international travel increases with more countries now re-opening their borders.
Image reference: 2021-11-17_weekly-tanker-time-charter-estimates-november-17-2021_img2.jpg

In the crude sector this week a small number of deals were reported, with fixtures ranging from four to six months to three years. The average rate for a VLCC for one-year has moved up slightly this week to an average of $23,500/pdpr.
After last week's positive sentiment, the clean market seems to have taken a breather in the last few days as news of new lockdowns in Europe cast uncertainty over oil demand.
Image reference: 2021-11-24_weekly-tanker-time-charter-estimates-november-24-2021_img2.jpg

Despite excitement over the last few weeks that the tanker market was finally recovering, the crude market remained subdued this week as concerns over the impact of the Omicron Covid variant halted any progression in the market.
Some period activity reported in the clean market this week as owners and charterers look to put together some fixtures before the holiday season begins.
Image reference: 2021-12-01_weekly-tanker-time-charter-estimates-december-01-2021_img2.jpg

A few timecharter fixtures reported in the crude sector this week, although we would expect more activityat this time of the year with owners and charterers usually keen to put deals together ahead of the Christmas period. However, uncertainty surrounding the new Omicron variant has left charterers unwilling to commit until there is more information about the severity of the new variant and the likely impact that this could have on demand.
On the MR side, the threat of new travel restrictions and further lockdowns has put pressure on the period market and as such there has been little reported in terms of timecharter activity. MR rates for one-year moved lower to an estimated $13,500/pdpr.
Image reference: 2021-12-08_weekly-tanker-time-charter-estimates-december-08-2021_img2.jpg

Very little to report in terms of timecharter activity in the crude sector this week, and rates have remained flat overall. However, news that China, the world's number one importer of crude, has ramped up its imports has been welcomed by investors. Imports were up in November to 10.17mbpd, a considerable jump from 8.9mbpd the previous month. The slump in China's crude imports in recent months has been one of the main pressures on the tanker market, particularly the VLCC sector.
A fairly lacklustre week for the clean sector with little timecharter activity reported as restrictions stemming from the latest Covid variant, particularly in Europe, continue to weigh heavily on demand.
Image reference: 2021-12-15_weekly-tanker-time-charter-estimates-december-15-2021_img2.jpg

We are still seeing some current activity emerge in s&p and timecharter markets, although now with Christmas almost upon us that has come to a close.
On the tanker clean side, sentiment has remained soft with slow activity throughout, as Omicron fears have stemmed travel plans over the festive season, cutting short-term demand.
Image reference: 2021-12-22_weekly-tanker-time-charter-estimates-december-22-2021_img2.jpg

Little change in the crude market at the start of the year, rates have remained steady although timecharter activity has been quiet so far.
The clean market has been quiet with limited enquiry although short term rates have moved up slightly. The average rate for an MR is estimated to be $13,350/pdpr.
Image reference: 2022-01-05_weekly-tanker-time-charter-estimates-january-05-2022_img2.jpg

Some activity reported in the crude market this week with a handful of suezmax period fixturesreported and also interest in a VLCC although Covid uncertainty continued to afflict the market and rate levels remain low.
On the clean side, there has been little to report in the way of fixtures this week and we see a continuation of this lull for the remainder of January.
Image reference: 2022-01-12_weekly-tanker-time-charter-estimates-january-12-2022_img2.jpg

in Weekly Tanker Time Charter Estimates 25/10/2023
Image reference: 2023-10-25_weekly-tanker-time-charter-estimates-october-25-2023_img2.jpg

in Recent Videos 24/05/2024
Key highlights from the Flex LNG Ltd Q1-2024 Result Presentation on May 23, 2024. The full presentation, includinga Q&A session, will be available on YouTube on May 23, 2024. Flex LNG Ltd is a shipping company focused on the growing market for Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) and is listed on NYSE and OSE under the ticker FLNG.
Source: Flex LNG

in Recent Videos 26/05/2024
KENC Engineering has successfully delivered a Flange Mounted Upending Tool [FMUT] to Van Oord.We designed, fabrication, tested and commissioned this crucial lifting tool for Van Oord to install monopiles in a UK offshore wind farm. It recently installed its first monopiles and operates smoothly and soundly. In this video, Jeroen Schiphorst (lead engineer at KENC), tells you all about the fabrication and testing process!
Source: KENC Engineering

in Recent Videos 25/06/2024
Video message from Arsenio Dominguez, IMO Secretary-General, on this year's campaign which looks at seafarers'contribution to making the maritime sector a safer workplace.
Source: IMOHQ

in Recent Videos 27/06/2024
Oceanos - NTUA was established in 2016 and comprises 70 undergraduate students from various institutions.Located at the NTUA campus, the team is organized into five sub-teams. They actively participated in the HydroContest for two years (2017-18) and have been involved in the MEBC since then. Their notable achievement includes securing 2nd place overall in 2023, with three 1st place wins. Beyond competition, their objectives extend to promoting the broader impact of green energy and contributing to a sustainable future.
Source: Yacht Club de Monaco

HELMEPA AT POSIDONIA 2024
Source: HELMEPA

in Recent Videos 16/07/2024
If a crane is ultimately a giant mechanical arm, the team at Lazaro Cardenas in Mexico have pumped their mechanical muscles to theextreme by perfecting a manoeuvre rarely achieved anywhere in the world - and scoring a first for Mexico: quad lifts with a ship-to-shore crane. Pulling off quad lifts - the lifting of four containers at a time - is an uncommon and highly skilled accomplishment. It's also not a task to be taken lightly. In port operations, where safety and speed are paramount, pushing the limits of efficiency necessitates careful calculation, intense planning and extraordinary teamwork.
Source: APM Terminals

Pulling off quad lifts - the lifting of four containers at a time - is an uncommon and highly skilled accomplishment. It's also not a task to be taken lightly. In port operations, where safety and speed are paramount, pushing the limits of efficiency necessitates careful calculation, intense planning and extraordinary teamwork.
Source: APM Terminals

in Recent Videos 30/09/2024
This briefing aimed to provide insights on the agenda for climate emissions and marine pollution discussions at the forthcoming82nd session of the International Maritime Organization's (IMO) Marine Environment Protection Committee (MEPC 82, 30 September - 4 October 2024). The event presented the Clean Arctic Alliance's expectations and recommendations for this meeting, and what is at stake for black carbon, ECAs, scrubbers, underwater noise, GHGs, plastics and biodiversity.
Source: Clean Arctic Alliance
tweet

This briefing aimed to provide insights on the agenda for climate emissions and marine pollution discussions at the forthcoming82nd session of the International Maritime Organization's (IMO) Marine Environment Protection Committee (MEPC 82, 30 September - 4 October 2024). The event presented the Clean Arctic Alliance's expectations and recommendations for this meeting, and what is at stake for black carbon, ECAs, scrubbers, underwater noise, GHGs, plastics and biodiversity.
Source: Clean Arctic Alliance
Black Carbon, Scrubbers & the Arctic: Clean Arctic Alliance Briefing for IMO PPR 12 Shipping Meeting

Ahead of the IMO's Pollution Prevention and Response sub-committee (PPR 12), which will meet from27-31 January 2025, this online briefing will provide insights on the agenda, likely discussions and possible outcomes of importance to ship operations in the Arctic, including reducing black carbon emissions and discharges from exhaust gas cleaning systems (scrubbers).
Speakers:
Bill Hemmings, Black Carbon Advisor: Reducing black carbon emissions
Eelco Leemans, Technical Advisor: Prohibiting the use of scrubbers
Sian Prior, Lead Advisor (Moderator)
Source: Clean Arctic Alliance
